feed.title: Euronews
feed.subtitle: Combined Euronews feed: News, My Europe, No Comment, Culture, Sport, Travel, and Next.

entry.title: 'The American Dream is back,' Trump says during Fourth of July speech
entry.content: "Our warriors did not fight communism on battlefields across the world only to have that menace rear its ugly head right back here in America," Trump told a cheering crowd.

entry.title: Serbs are not 'little Russians', says former PM Ana Brnabić
entry.content: Serbia's former prime minister tells Euronews how Belgrade has backed Ukraine since the war began and rejects claims of being pro-Russian, while voicing Serbs' frustration at EU accession double standards.

entry.title: Fireworks light up Washington sky for America's 250th anniversary
entry.content: A fireworks display illuminated the National Mall in Washington as the United States marked the 250th anniversary of its independence.

entry.title: 'I believe in the secret of things': Author Orhan Pamuk opens up at the Museum of Innocence
entry.content: In an exclusive interview at the house in Çukurcuma which Orhan Pamuk turned into a museum, the Nobel Prize-winning author talked about the power of objects to bring back memories, the shocking nature of collecting and the legacy of the museum as a city memory for the future.

entry.title: Stockholm travel guide: Sunsets, fika and island swimming in the stunning archipelago
entry.content: If you’re looking for the coolest of coolcations, then a visit to Sweden’s capital city should be on your travel bucket list. In this guide to Stockholm, Mike Starling goes chasing sunsets, gets a taste of fika, and goes wild swimming on a charming island in the archipelago.

entry.title: Latest news bulletin | July 5th, 2026 – Morning
entry.content: Catch up with the most important stories from around Europe and beyond this July 5th, 2026 - latest news, breaking news, World, Business, Entertainment, Politics, Culture, Travel.

entry.title: Morocco beats World Cup co-host Canada 3-0 and advances to quarterfinals
entry.content: It’s Morocco’s second straight appearance in the quarterfinals after becoming the first African team to reach the semifinals in 2022.

entry.title: 80 vials of fentanyl stolen from Rome hospital, enough for 20,000 doses
entry.content: 80 vials have disappeared from the capital's Jewish hospital pharmacy, with no sign of a break‑in. The synthetic opioid, 100 times stronger than morphine and a national emergency in North America, sparks an emergency meeting at Palazzo Chigi.

entry.title: Diamonds are forever: Karlovy Vary film festival honours Dustin Hoffman on its 60th anniversary
entry.content: The largest cultural event in the Czech Republic, the Karlovy Vary international film festival is just getting underway with the famous spa town celebrating its diamond 60th edition. Jiri Skacel delves into the festival's past to explain its increasing popularity among younger cinephiles.

entry.title: Thousands of protesters march throughout France to demand new law against sexual violence
entry.content: The rape and murder of 11-year-old Lyhanna in France has become a catalyst for demands to combat sexual violence against children with a "comprehensive framework law" that covers prevention and judicial treatment as well as support for victims.

entry.title: Latest news bulletin | July 4th, 2026 – Evening
entry.content: Catch up with the most important stories from around Europe and beyond this July 4th, 2026 - latest news, breaking news, World, Business, Entertainment, Politics, Culture, Travel.

entry.title: 400,000-year-old intact cave found in Israel sheds light on pre-Neanderthal era
entry.content: A prehistoric cave found near Foreidis in northern Israel is offering a rare glimpse into a little-known phase of human evolution. Sealed for hundreds of thousands of years, the site preserves tools, animal remains and evidence of prolonged occupation.

entry.title: World leaders and royals celebrate the US's 250th birthday
entry.content: "The story of the relationship between our two nations is one of extraordinary evolution, from overcoming conflict to forging one of the closest and most productive alliances the world has seen," King Charles III said.

entry.title: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce marry at Madison Square Garden in private but lavish wedding
entry.content: The wedding, officiated by Adam Sandler, was attended by roughly 1,000 guests, including many A-list celebrities, singers, athletes and close friends and family.

entry.title: Keir Starmer calls decision to resign as British PM 'intensely personal'
entry.content: Starmer also said the next prime minister would not be able to spend any less time focusing on diplomacy and international affairs, citing the impact of the wars in Ukraine and Iran on the cost of living.

entry.title: Pope makes historic visit to Lampedusa, warns Europe: 'Deaths at sea are failures to act'
entry.content: Pope Francis has visited the island that symbolises the migrant crisis. Greeted by thousands, he stressed Europe’s duty to act and urged the faithful not to ignore the suffering of those forced to leave their homeland.

entry.title: Ukraine strikes oil infrastructure sites near Saint Petersburg, Zelenskyy says
entry.content: It comes after Moscow launched a massive drone and missile barrage at Kyiv earlier this week, killing at least 30 people and hitting more than 20 sites across the city.

entry.title: Trump warns of communist threat as Washington parade cancelled due to heatwave
entry.content: "Communism is a mortal threat to American liberty," Trump said during a speech on Friday. "It is the greatest threat to our country".

entry.title: Latest news bulletin | July 4th, 2026 – Midday
entry.content: Catch up with the most important stories from around Europe and beyond this July 4th, 2026 - latest news, breaking news, World, Business, Entertainment, Politics, Culture, Travel.

entry.title: Second World War gun recovered from river in Germany
entry.content: The town of Forst in Lower Lusatia was almost completely destroyed during heavy fighting between the German Wehrmacht and the Red Army in April 1945.

entry.title: Iranians mourn as Khamenei funeral ceremonies begin
entry.content: Large crowds gathered in Tehran as Iran began six days of funeral ceremonies for late Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei, who was killed during the US-Israeli conflict.

entry.title: 20,000 protest against AfD party conference in Germany
entry.content: Thüringen police described the protest as "legitimate" but said an AfD constituency office and police officers had been attacked with paint bombs and fireworks.

entry.title: Cape Verde fans stand proud despite Argentina defeat
entry.content: Cape Verde supporters expressed pride in their team's historic World Cup campaign despite a 3-2 extra-time defeat to defending champions Argentina.

entry.title: 'Vanishingly rare' copy of US Declaration of Independence discovered in the UK
entry.content: Graham Moore, the Revolution 250 curator at the National Archives, said the "exceptional" item was "one of the rarest forms of the Declaration we know about".

entry.title: NATO's drone problem: Can European industry close the gap?
entry.content: At NATO AIRCOM's Industry Day in Ramstein, Euronews had exclusive access, speaking to military leaders and defence companies about the race to develop cheaper, faster and more effective ways to counter drones.

entry.title: Argentina survive major World Cup scare as Egypt edge past Australia
entry.content: World Cup newcomers Cape Verde scored one of the goals of the tournament against Argentina in Miami.

entry.title: Meet My Mama: how home cooks turned chefs are spicing up the French catering market
entry.content: “My cuisine is a bridge between Congo, my home country, and France,” says Marie-Clarisse, a home cook turned professional chef by Meet My Mama, a French start-up that's serving up fusion food for world class events. Valérie Gauriat discovers the recipe of their success.

entry.title: Plovdiv travel guide: How to spend 48 hours in Europe’s oldest continuously inhabited city
entry.content: In this travel guide to Plovdiv, discover Roman sites, Bulgarian Revival houses and the best restaurants and bars.

entry.title: Latest news bulletin | July 4th, 2026 – Morning
entry.content: Catch up with the most important stories from around Europe and beyond this July 4th, 2026 - latest news, breaking news, World, Business, Entertainment, Politics, Culture, Travel.

entry.title: Ireland, as 'honest broker', reckons with the high price of dirty business
entry.content: Ireland is facing the music over its continued sales of alumina to Russia. The scandal put a damper on the start of the country's EU presidency and raised uncomfortable questions that Dublin has yet to answer.

entry.title: World's largest digital camera starts mapping the universe
entry.content: The Rubin observatory in Chile has begun its decade survey of the sky, snapping hundreds of images, to help researchers understand dark matter.

entry.title: A Silk Road destination wants tourists to stay longer
entry.content: Tourist trips to Uzbekistan rose from 6.63 million in 2023 to 11.7 million in 2025. Authorities are linking that growth to new air routes, Central Asian travel packages and higher tourism revenue.

entry.title: Lefebvrists answer Pope after excommunication as Redemptorist schism looms
entry.content: The Lefebvrists of the Society of St Pius X reject the Holy See's excommunication after consecrating bishops without Leo XIV's approval. Now it's the turn of the Transalpine Redemptorists: another episcopal consecration without papal mandate has been announced in Scotland.

entry.title: Prospective UK Prime Minister Andy Burnham rules out early general election
entry.content: Burnham indicated he still backs reforming Britain's first-past-the-post electoral system, which has historically benefited the country's traditional two main parties.

entry.title: Conservative candidate Keiko Fujimori wins Peru's presidential election in runoff
entry.content: Voters were primarily concerned with increasing levels of crime, especially extortion by violent organized crime gangs, and Fujimori pledged to combat crime with an iron fist.

entry.title: Did he say 'Bismillah'? Cristiano Ronaldo’s penalty whisper ignites global debate
entry.content: Superstar striker Cristiano Ronaldo’s whisper broadcast live around the world before the decisive penalty against Croatia sparked a global debate over whether it was an Arabic-inspired sacred invocation or his own pre-Saudi encouragement routine in Portuguese.

entry.title: Two Romanian men jailed by UK judge over London stabbing of Iranian TV presenter
entry.content: Iran International, a private broadcaster based in a heavily secured building in west London, was labelled a "terrorist" organisation by Tehran in 2022 along with the BBC's Persian-language channel.

entry.title: EBRD backs Kazakhstan's move up the minerals value chain with €255m loan
entry.content: The European Bank for Reconstruction and Development (EBRD) is financing Kazakhstan's first large-scale refractory gold ore processing facility, helping the country unlock previously inaccessible reserves while expanding domestic mineral processing.

entry.title: Belgian diamond group that won tariff relief gifts Trump lavishly encrusted ring
entry.content: US presidents have considerable discretion to accept gifts from domestic and foreign sources and may determine themselves whether a gift was meant for them personally or the nation.

entry.title: Latest news bulletin | July 3rd, 2026 – Evening
entry.content: Catch up with the most important stories from around Europe and beyond this July 3rd, 2026 - latest news, breaking news, World, Business, Entertainment, Politics, Culture, Travel.

entry.title: Mourners in Damascus bury cafe bomb blast victims as officials promise justice
entry.content: Although there is so far no indication who carried out the attack, many were quick to blame loyalists of former President Bashar al-Assad, who was ousted in an insurgent offensive in December 2024.

entry.title: European Commission attempts to spur EU defence teamwork with five new major projects
entry.content: In a bid to end the problem of European Union nations procuring defence capabilities separately, the European Commission is trying to kickstart momentum with the participation of 18 member states – and Ukraine.

entry.title: Extreme weather, conflicts and responsible tourism: How travel is changing in 2026
entry.content: Destinations will need to strengthen their ability to anticipate and adapt to uncertainty, according to a new report.

entry.title: Iran FM confirms reports Israel planned to target Tehran's negotiators
entry.content: Iran's foreign minister corroborated US media reports that Washington warned Tehran Israel may have had its chief negotiators on a target list during April talks in Islamabad, the same talks that produced the framework deal signed on 17 June.

entry.title: 'Security of the Baltics is our security': Merz meets Baltic leaders before NATO summit
entry.content: Days before the NATO summit in Ankara, Germany’s chancellor and Baltic leaders in Berlin pledged stronger deterrence against Russia and more aid for Ukraine.

entry.title: NATO to reaffirm iron-clad commitment to Article 5 at Ankara summit
entry.content: As NATO leaders prepare for their summit in Ankara next week, a document seen by Euronews says all 32 members – including the US – are still committed to the alliance's principle of collective defence.

entry.title: NASA sends robot into orbit to stop telescope crashing to Earth
entry.content: Swift has been circling Earth for 21 years. Without a rescue mission assembled in just nine months, it would burn up by October.

entry.title: EU will retaliate if China misses October trade deadline, von der Leyen says
entry.content: "We are basically prepared for everything, and we have all the instruments on the table," Ursula von der Leyen said, hinting at retaliation against China. The EU has imposed an October deadline to secure tangible results.

entry.title: EU blacklists six people involved in Navalny assassination
entry.content: Six Russian scientists linked to Alexei Navalny's epibatidine poisoning now face asset freezes and EU-wide travel bans.

entry.title: Humanoid robots showcase football skills
entry.content: Humanoid robots compete in football matches at RoboCup in Incheon, South Korea, showcasing advances in robotics as spectators watch the action.

entry.title: Overcrowded bus plunges into ravine in southwest Pakistan, killing 40 passengers
entry.content: Pakistan’s President Asif Ali Zardari expressed sorrow over the crash, offered condolences to the families of those killed and wished the injured a speedy recovery.

entry.title: Ukraine to call on NATO partners to deliver air defence after brutal Russian bombardment
entry.content: Ukraine is calling on its partners to deliver air defence systems to Ukraine after a Russian aerial attack killed at least 30 people on 2 July. With NATO allies preparing to meet in Ankara, Kyiv is urging them not to delay what they have already promised.

entry.title: The ‘Wonderwall’ effect: Have England finally got a decent World Cup anthem?
entry.content: The team's travelling football supporters have taken to singing ‘Wonderwall’ by Oasis with the players at full-time. Now, streams are up and a new tradition is born. Can it last?

entry.title: 'This passion makes me feel alive': Tango finds an unexpected welcome in Azerbaijan's capital city
entry.content: You might not expect to feel the pulse of Argentinian music echoing through the narrow streets of Baku, but the Azeri capital's inner city, Icherisheher, has been transformed by a Tango-loving dance troupe

entry.title: Video shows moment 11-year-old drives pickup truck into Buddhist procession
entry.content: Ten people were killed after an 11-year-old boy drove a pickup truck into a Buddhist procession in northeastern Thailand.

entry.title: How a NATO essay contest won a Lithuanian schoolgirl a ride in a Bundeswehr tank
entry.content: As Germany prepares to permanently station 5,000 troops in Lithuania, one schoolgirl has already experienced the country's future brigade firsthand after winning a ride in a Bundeswehr tank.

entry.title: Ukraine frontline frozen in June as Russian momentum falters, June data shows
entry.content: Hundreds of thousands have been killed and millions forced from their homes since Russia invaded in February 2022.

entry.title: A year with zero deaths on its streets: How Helsinki became a world reference for road safety
entry.content: With around 1.4 million inhabitants, the Helsinki metropolitan area has achieved an unquestionable milestone in road safety. What can other EU capitals and big cities learn from its success story?

entry.title: Lagarde mulls leaving ECB early to play a role in French elections
entry.content: European Central Bank President Christine Lagarde said she is considering the possibility of taking part in the upcoming French elections, a move that could result in her stepping down from her post earlier than planned.

entry.title: Iran to host dozens of foreign leaders for Khamenei's funeral, with Western nations absent
entry.content: Russia's Medvedev, Pakistan's prime minister and Taliban officials are among roughly 30 country delegations expected as Iran buries slain Ayatollah Ali Khamenei this week.

entry.title: Interpol issues red notice for Ukrainian woman over Monaco bomb attack
entry.content: The alert says Berezovska speaks German, has dark hair, and has a tattoo - possibly of a snake - on her upper right arm.

entry.title: Tripadvisor AI summaries give glowing reviews to dangerous hotels, consumer watchdog finds
entry.content: Tripadvisor's new AI review summaries are masking reports of food poisoning, sexual harassment and serious hygiene failures, a Which? investigation has found.

entry.title: Amazon set to launch Leo, its rival to SpaceX's Starlink internet, later this year
entry.content: Amazon has deployed enough satellites to begin offering its Leo internet service this year, marking its most significant step yet in the race to take on SpaceX's Starlink.

entry.title: Ukraine and Moldova set to open another cluster of EU accession talks
entry.content: EU countries have unanimously agreed to start the process to open another accession cluster with Ukraine and Moldova, the one on external relations, with a formal ceremony expected on 14 July.

entry.title: What is GLM 5.2? The new Chinese AI model that’s rivalling Anthropic
entry.content: China's GLM-5.2 rivals Claude and GPT-5.5 and was released a day after the US export ban on Anthropic models.

entry.title: October deadline for China talks 'not realistic at all', chief trade MEP says
entry.content: Bernd Lange, head of the European Parliament's trade committee, told Euronews the EU should be pushing China hard on unfair industrial subsidies and other pressing issues.

entry.title: Euronews Culture's Film of the Week: 'Fatherland' - Paweł Pawlikowski's Cannes-winning triumph
entry.content: Paweł Pawlikowski's first film in eight years stars Sandra Hüller and Hanns Zischler as Erika and Thomas Mann, as they embark on a road trip across Germany during the Cold War.

entry.title: Moldovan prime minister Alexandru Munteanu resigns after eight months in office
entry.content: Moldova's president Maia Sandu said she would start consultations with the country's political parties on Monday over selecting a new prime minister.

entry.title: US Democrats urge Brussels to stick to methane rules
entry.content: US lawmakers have presented a united front in defence of predictable market rules, arguing that any rollback of methane regulations would send a damaging signal to global investors who have already invested in CO2 emissions monitoring technologies.

entry.title: Madonna takes us to Danceteria with her 'Confessions II' album: Euronews Culture's verdict
entry.content: Madonna releases her highly-anticipated album 'Confessions II', a spiritual sequel to 2005's 'Confessions on a Dance Floor'. Madge fan Mikhail Calvez delves into the tracklist and assesses whether the dance floor is still alive in 2026.

entry.title: Venezuela's interim leader defends earthquake response as death toll tops 2,500
entry.content: Delcy Rodríguez has hit back at criticism over her government’s response to the quakes, rejecting accusations of slow rescue efforts and claims that official casualty figures have been under-reported.

entry.title: Dogs don Argentina shirts as World Cup fever grips Buenos Aires
entry.content: A dog walker in Buenos Aires leads dogs dressed in Argentina jerseys through the city as World Cup fever grows and fans celebrate in the streets.

entry.title: Latest news bulletin | July 3rd, 2026 – Midday
entry.content: Catch up with the most important stories from around Europe and beyond this July 3rd, 2026 - latest news, breaking news, World, Business, Entertainment, Politics, Culture, Travel.

entry.title: Viral fake AI-image shows Macron kneeling before Thai king
entry.content: Did French President Emmanuel Macron kneel before Thailand's King Maha Vajiralongkorn? Despite online claims, this viral image is fake.

entry.title: Cyprus: Only 23% of tourist accommodation is fully licensed, deputy tourism minister responds
entry.content: The report by Cyprus’s Audit Office says 55% of accommodation operates without full legality, while a further 22% only hold a temporary licence.

entry.title: This steam railway that travels through the Swiss Alps is celebrating its 100th anniversary
entry.content: The steam trains now operate exclusively as a tourist attraction in the summers between the stations of Realp and Oberwald.

entry.title: Rescued bile bears released in Denmark forest
entry.content: Six rescued Asiatic black bears from South Korea start a new life in Denmark’s Knuthenborg Safaripark after an international rescue operation.

entry.title: Aegean resumes direct flights between Paphos and Athens
entry.content: At 6:31 am local time, the first Aegean flight arrived in Athens, landing at the Greek capital’s airport in the early hours of Friday morning.

entry.title: Lithuania moves to lift nuclear weapons ban, following Finland's lead
entry.content: President Gitanas Nauseda said Lithuania's constitutional ban on nuclear weapons and foreign military bases had become "outdated," as Finland's own nuclear ban formally ended on Wednesday, prompting Russian threats that Moscow could destroy half the country.

entry.title: Air conditioner rush: Scuffles break out at Lidl stores across France
entry.content: Hundreds of shoppers queued outside Lidl supermarkets across France on Thursday after the retailer announced the sale of 200,000 fans and portable air conditioners, triggering long lines, scuffles and police intervention at several stores.

entry.title: AI images falsely claim Kyiv monastery strike was staged
entry.content: Posts circulating in several languages falsely claim that photographers were waiting on rooftops to capture the moment a strike hit Kyiv’s UNESCO-listed Perchersk Lavra monastery.

entry.title: De Croo: Defence and aid spending are 'two sides of the same coin'
entry.content: Exclusive: United Nations Development Programme administrator and former Belgian Prime Minister Alexander De Croo said the European Union's long-term budget should balance defence and aid funding as they are both crucial for European security.

entry.title: Podcast | US Independence party in Brussels: Controversial celebration or diplomatic success?
entry.content: A majestic celebration of the US Independence anniversary in the heart of Brussels' European Quarter, alongside talks to end the war in Iran have sparked reflection on EU–US relations.

entry.title: 'Donald Trump's always raging about something,' former US NATO envoy says
entry.content: Kurt Volker told Europe Today the US president's anger at allies for not joining in the war in Iran was an "exaggeration".

entry.title: Portugal fans celebrate World Cup win over Croatia
entry.content: Thousands of Portugal fans gathered in central Lisbon to celebrate a 2-1 victory over Croatia, sending their team through to the World Cup last 16.

entry.title: Goalkeeper Unai Simón sets new Guinness World Record following Spain's 3-0 win over Austria
entry.content: Simón, who plays his club football for La Liga side Athletic Bilbao, has kept clean sheets against Austria, Cape Verde, Uruguay, and Saudi Arabia at the 2026 World Cup.

entry.title: Overtourism: Where are locals being priced out by rising rents?
entry.content: Greeks, Italians and Spaniards have endured some of the highest rent hikes since 2019, ranging from €200 to €300 a year, and no, it has nothing to do with energy or construction prices.

entry.title: Firefighters battle major wildfire in southern France
entry.content: A major wildfire in France's Aude department burns 900 hectares as aircraft and firefighters battle the blaze and evacuees reach emergency shelters.

entry.title: Watch: Following the money—the EPPO investigation into defunct far-right EU group
entry.content: The dark times are ahead as the EU is coming for the far-right. But this time, it is not about a political debate—it is about the balance sheet.

entry.title: Newsletter: ‘Raging’ Trump turns up the heat ahead of NATO summit
entry.content: Good morning and welcome to Friday, I’m Mared Gwyn. We wrap up this week with another dispatch from Ireland as it takes on the six-month EU Council Presidency, and a look ahead to next week’s high-stakes NATO summit in Ankara.

entry.title: A-listers, phone bans, charity donations: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce wedding frenzy in full swing
entry.content: Taylor Swift and her American football-player fiancé Travis Kelce have kicked off rumoured wedding celebrations at Madison Square Garden in New York City. Here’s what we know so far.

entry.title: Death toll rises to 30 following Russia's massive attack on Kyiv
entry.content: The Ukrainian branch of the Red Cross said its humanitarian warehouse in Kyiv had been destroyed in the strikes, with around €1.5 million worth of aid and equipment lost.

entry.title: Israelis mark 1,000 days since 7 October attack
entry.content: Thousands gathered in Tel Aviv to mark 1,000 days since the 7 October 2023 attack, as former hostages and bereaved families addressed the crowd.

entry.title: Europe Today: Kyiv attack, NATO summit and pressure on Russia
entry.content: Ukraine observes a day of mourning after Russia's deadly strike on Kyiv as NATO allies prepare for next week's summit in Ankara. Ireland considers ending alumina exports to Russia while the EU investigates the Patriots political group.

entry.title: Das Mühlwald review: Could a family-friendly resort make my toddler sleep through the night?
entry.content: Described as South Tyrol's only quality-time family resort, does Das Mühlwald live up to its promise? It certainly did, says Ruth Wright in her review, as the property ‘gave us space to be a family in all our loud, exhausted, unpredictable messiness’.

entry.title: Data protection rules slow LLM rollout in Europe, study says
entry.content: A new Governance AI study reveals that EU data protection rules are stalling AI adoption, leaving 11% of advanced LLM releases delayed or blocked in Europe compared to the US.

entry.title: Latest news bulletin | July 3rd, 2026 – Morning
entry.content: Catch up with the most important stories from around Europe and beyond this July 3rd, 2026 - latest news, breaking news, World, Business, Entertainment, Politics, Culture, Travel.

entry.title: Brussels pivots from climate mitigation to adaptation as heatwaves expose vulnerabilities
entry.content: After a brutal heatwave killed 1,300 people in western Europe, the European Commission has pledged to step up climate adaptation efforts – but part of the burden was shifted to local and regional governments.

entry.title: 6.2 earthquake hits eastern Indonesia
entry.content: The tremor hit at a depth of 120 kilometres, roughly 58 kilometres west of Tobelo in North Maluku province, and officials confirmed there is no tsunami threat.

entry.title: Spain cruise past Austria while Portugal edge Croatia Wednesday to book last-16 tie
entry.content: Spain blanked Austria 3–0 in California with a brace from Mikel Oyarzabal and a Pedro Porro header. They will meet Portugal, who edged Croatia 2–1 in Toronto thanks to a late Gonçalo Ramos header.

entry.title: Trump says 'ridiculous' for US to maintain current support for NATO
entry.content: US President Donald Trump said it is "ridiculous" for the United States to continue its "one-sided" relationship with NATO.

entry.title: Turkish comedian arrested for 'insulting' Islam and Erdogan
entry.content: Turkish comedian Deniz Göktaş was arrested at Istanbul Airport on Thursday following a stand-up show posted to YouTube. The 32-year-old faces multiple probes, including "publicly insulting religious values" and insulting President Recep Tayyip Erdoğan.

entry.title: Suspect identified in Monaco parcel bombing, prosecutors say
entry.content: The attack shocked the country on the Mediterranean coast, one of the world’s smallest sovereign states known for its high concentration of wealthy residents.

entry.title: Former Olympian indicted on felony charge over alleged Reflecting Pool vandalism
entry.content: District of Columbia US Attorney Jeanine Pirro said David Hearn ripped up recently installed sealant on pool in “a deliberate act” that caused more than €875 in damage.

entry.title: Lídia Jorge wins 2026 Camões Prize
entry.content: The honour comes a year after she was invited by the President of the Republic to speak on Portugal Day, following major awards in France and Austria.

entry.title: Daredevil couple charged with felonies for Empire State Building proposal stunt
entry.content: The highest public floor is the 102nd, where there’s an observation deck. Going higher requires a key card, according to the court complaint.

entry.title: Eight arrested in probe into men drugging and raping partners, UK crime agency says
entry.content: As part of a separate operation into such offences, EU law enforcement agency Europol said the group of countries had identified 156 victims and perpetrators of drug-facilitated sexual assaults.

entry.title: Wildfires rage in southern France prompting evacuation of 3,000 people
entry.content: The Meteo-France weather agency said a new spell of elevated temperatures would hit the country next week, although they are not expected to be extreme like in June.

entry.title: Venezuelan security guard pulled alive from basement eight days after twin quakes
entry.content: The earthquakes destroyed tens of thousands of buildings across northern Venezuela, killing more than 2,200 people, and leaving La Guaira state as the hardest-hit region in the country.

entry.title: Von der Leyen unveils major EU support initiatives in strategic trip to Armenia
entry.content: European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen announced a significant economic and political support package to Armenia during her strategic visit to engage with the South Caucasus peace process and the new opportunities in the region formerly in Russia’s orbit.

entry.title: UN to close its human rights office in Burkina Faso following junta's suspension
entry.content: Junta chief captain Ibrahim Traoré has pursued anti-Western policies since seizing power in a coup in September 2022, while critical voices are met with repression.

entry.title: 'No cherry-picking': Pressure mounts on Ireland to stop alumina sales to Russia
entry.content: The scandal over alumina sales to Russia continues to dog Ireland as it takes over the rotating presidency of the EU Council, but Dublin says it will not commit to sanctions until an internal investigation is concluded.

entry.title: Russia steps up use of jet-powered drones beyond the reach of Ukrainian interceptors
entry.content: Launched in hundreds within just a few hours, attack drones play a key role in Russia’s aerial campaign against Ukraine. Moscow is now increasingly using jet-powered drones, which are much harder to intercept.

entry.title: French aerobatic team stages flypast over New York for US anniversary
entry.content: Patrouille de France flies over New York with Alpha Jets and coloured smoke during celebrations marking the 250th anniversary of the US Declaration of Independence.

entry.title: Latest news bulletin | July 2nd, 2026 – Evening
entry.content: Catch up with the most important stories from around Europe and beyond this July 2nd, 2026 - latest news, breaking news, World, Business, Entertainment, Politics, Culture, Travel.

entry.title: EU institutions pile on pressure for 'ambitious' budget with new resources
entry.content: As member states negotiate a €2 trillion long-term budget, the European Commission is advocating for tangible proposals on new revenues, known as "own resources".

entry.title: Leaked EU document confirms Hungarian spy ring, renews pressure on Commissioner Várhelyi
entry.content: A leaked European Commission document indicates that Hungary operated a diplomatic intelligence network targeting EU staff from 2012 to 2018, reviving scrutiny of Commissioner Olivér Várhelyi and calls for his resignation.

entry.title: Iran renews Hormuz threats as Doha talks end with cautious progress
entry.content: Tehran threatened a "forceful response" against vessels straying from its approved Hormuz routes, even as mediators said indirect US-Iran talks in Doha concluded with "positive progress". The next round of talks will wait until after Ayatollah Ali Khamenei's funeral, which begins on Saturday.

entry.title: Hitmen took €150,000 to murder journalist Daphne Caruana Galizia, Malta court hears
entry.content: Caruana Galizia had exposed corruption at the highest level in the country, shining a spotlight on murky links between Malta's business and political elites.

entry.title: Almost 1.2 million apply for Spain's migrant regularisation scheme, official figures show
entry.content: Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez has touted the benefits of immigration and the vast regularisation scheme for sectors such as construction that need to boost their workforce.

entry.title: Albanian police fire water cannon as protests against Trump-linked luxury resort continue
entry.content: The government says the development on the Adriatic coast would be transformational for the former communist nation as it seeks to enter the high-end tourism market and pushes for European Union membership.

entry.title: How to fly to Asia from Europe for less: All the budget airline routes to know about
entry.content: In this guide, we’re helping you discover the most affordable flight routes between Europe and Asia.

entry.title: Brussels supports air conditioning—but not as the main answer
entry.content: Cooling Europe's cities will require a combination of measures, with air conditioning serving as just one element of a broader climate adaptation strategy, one EU official said.

entry.title: EU car industry clashes over strategy to fight Chinese competitors
entry.content: With thousands of jobs are at stake, EU car suppliers and manufacturers disagree over the local content requirement set by the European Commission in its Made in Europe bill.

entry.title: Keir Starmer issues state apology for decades of forced adoptions practices in UK
entry.content: Previously, the then-Conservative government declined to apologise, arguing that the state had not supported these practices. Starmer has now acknowledged the government's role in forced adoptions.

entry.title: Germany's Merz announces sweeping reforms: Tax cuts, pension overhaul and new sick leave rules
entry.content: After seven hours of talks, the CDU/CSU-SPD coalition agreed on sweeping tax, labour and pension reforms, a breakthrough aimed at reviving the struggling economy and countering the rise of the far right.

entry.title: Damascus explosion caused by bomb, state TV says, as death toll climbs to five
entry.content: The explosion comes a day after the first parliament since the fall of long-time leader Bashar al-Assad began to take shape.

entry.title: Ukraine calls for air defences as Russia threatens more strikes on Kyiv after overnight attack
entry.content: After an overnight assault that killed at least 20 people in Ukraine, the Kremlin stated it will increase the pace of its attacks to achieve its goals.

entry.title: OpenAI offers the US government a 5% ownership stake
entry.content: Sam Altman reportedly wants to hand the US government a stake in OpenAI worth tens of billions, and he wants Silicon Valley's other giants to do the same.

entry.title: Death threats and presidential condemnation: Explaining the anger over South Korea’s World Cup exit
entry.content: South Korea's World Cup came to an end last weekend, but the nation's anger continues to boil over, threatening to bring the culture of football into further disrepute. And expose a corruption case.

entry.title: 11-year-old boy dies from rabies in Canada after waking up to a bat on his face
entry.content: Rabies is a rare but deadly infection usually spread by a bite or scratch from an infected animal. It is almost always fatal after the onset of symptoms, although vaccination and early treatment can help to prevent it.

entry.title: What's behind the viral claims of child trafficking on Vinted?
entry.content: Millions have viewed videos claiming that children are being sold through coded advertisements on the popular second-hand online marketplace Vinted. What's behind the rumours?

entry.title: This is how to get cheap last-minute flights, according to Skyscanner
entry.content: The flight comparison website has shared seven top tips for nabbing a deal.

entry.title: Any deal with Tehran is 'a pause before the next war,' Reza Pahlavi tells Euronews
entry.content: In an exclusive interview with Euronews, Reza Pahlavi said the framework agreement would allow the Islamic Republic to regroup and rearm, with the only winner from the deal being the regime in Tehran.

entry.title: Fast-moving wildfire spreads across southern France
entry.content: Firefighters battle a fast-moving wildfire in Aude, southern France, after flames spread from Hérault and burned more than 800 hectares overnight.

entry.title: Tavolara glamping: Government revokes special economic zone approval in Sardinia, no luxury resort
entry.content: A luxury resort financed by Brazilian investors was planned, with special economic zone status to bypass landscape and environmental rules. Civic and environmental groups forced the government to revoke the permits, and the regional authority has appealed to the administrative court.

entry.title: How the Entry-Exit System is becoming a nightmare for Europe's summer travellers
entry.content: The digital system for checking non-EU travellers’ documents is creating bottlenecks and glitches just as the peak of the holiday season approaches. As queues grow longer and passengers get more frustrated, airlines are calling for the option to suspend the checks in July and August.

entry.title: 'Are we serious about war?': EU defence chief calls on countries to live up to security ambitions
entry.content: The Defence Commissioner warned that member states cannot bang the drum for a stronger security architecture while cutting the EU budget, and urged spending that matches their new defence ambitions.

entry.title: Window to control AI is closing and it could widen inequality, UN experts warn
entry.content: UN warns AI could widen the gap between rich and poor countries in a new report.

entry.title: Vatican excommunicates Lefebvrists over unauthorised ordinations
entry.content: It comes after the controversial ordination of four new bishops carried out without papal approval.

entry.title: Latest news bulletin | July 2nd, 2026 – Midday
entry.content: Catch up with the most important stories from around Europe and beyond this July 2nd, 2026 - latest news, breaking news, World, Business, Entertainment, Politics, Culture, Travel.

entry.title: Jurassic fashion? T-Rex leather could reshape luxury industry says VML's Dimitri Guerassimov
entry.content: To create the leather, scientists started with fragments of fossilised T-Rex collagen, then used AI and computational biology to reconstruct the missing genetic code.

entry.title: EU targets Russian drone-makers with sanctions following massive strikes on Kyiv
entry.content: The new sanctions will target Russia's military-industrial complex, including firms that manufacture Shahed and Geran drone components.

entry.title: Bulgaria aims for 'no further cuts on cohesion' in EU's next budget, minister says
entry.content: Bulgaria's Deputy Prime Minister Atanas Pekanov said the EU must "retain policies like cohesion" in its next long-term budget. While acknowledging negotiations "will take months", he backed reaching a deal by the end of the year, saying it is "better to reach a solution fast".

entry.title: Smoke rises over Kyiv after Russian overnight strikes
entry.content: Smoke rises over Kyiv after a large-scale Russian missile and drone attack overnight that killed at least eight people and injured dozens.

entry.title: Kazakhstan starts living by new Constitution, sets date for parliamentary elections
entry.content: Kazakhstan's new Constitution entered into force on July 1, prompting the Kazakh President to set August 23 as the date for parliamentary elections required under the new constitutional framework.

entry.title: EU Court of Justice rejects Google’s appeal against record €4.1 billion fine
entry.content: Google CEO Sundar Pichai has argued that the company's Android system has created "more choice, not less".

entry.title: 'Terrible judgement': Queen Camilla faces backlash after royal meeting with J.K. Rowling
entry.content: Queen Camilla recently spent time with J.K. Rowling to mark the start of Royal Week in Scotland. This has sparked criticism, leading many to call out the Royal Family for displaying poor judgement - especially at the end of Pride month.

entry.title: US fans celebrate World Cup last-16 spot
entry.content: US supporters celebrate in Carson, California, after the United States beat Bosnia-Herzegovina 2-0 to reach the World Cup round of 16.

entry.title: Dream job alert: Get paid to travel around Europe talking to strangers
entry.content: Self-improvement app RiseGuide is hiring a ‘Global Charisma Scout’ who will create an index of where on the continent it’s easiest to strike up a conversation.

entry.title: 'Major health crisis looming over Venezuela,' EU Civil Protection expert warns
entry.content: An EU expert who is on the ground in Venezuela to coordinate the bloc's response to the earthquake told Euronews that "the clock is ticking", adding that the nature of the support provided will shift to focus on medical assistance.

entry.title: EU Budget Commissioner Piotr Serafin criticises ‘frugals’ over proposed long-term budget cuts
entry.content: The so-called "frugal" member states are asking a significant reduction in spending ahead of the EU's upcoming 2028-2034 budget.

entry.title: Fast fashion: 69% of our clothes contain toxic chemicals
entry.content: The European Union is implementing a new tax on imports, to limit purchases from fast-fashion e-platforms like SHEIN, Temu, and similar. Their bargain price tags hide a toxic reality, as testing reveals these ultra-cheap garments carry a hidden cost to human health.

entry.title: How NASA, Microsoft and the EU use AI to speed up post-quake rescues in Venezuela
entry.content: Artificial intelligence is now a key ally for emergency crews. Space agencies, tech firms and international bodies use satellite images and AI to map the areas hardest hit by the quake.

entry.title: Trump was paid over $10 million by Amazon for 'Melania' documentary
entry.content: Trump’s cryptocurrency earnings were disclosed this week, leading to scathing criticism online. Part of the financial disclosures reveal how much he was paid by Amazon for this year’s Melania documentary.

entry.title: Alleged member of hacking group 'Scattered Spider' arrested in Finland
entry.content: Scattered Spider, also known as “Octo Tempest,” “UNC3944,” or “0ktapus,” is a group of criminal cyber actors that attempts to extort ransoms from companies.

entry.title: Couple climbs Empire State Building, gets engaged, then arrested
entry.content: Two climbers scaled the Empire State Building's antenna, displayed a peace banner and appeared to get engaged before being arrested by New York police.

entry.title: Ahead of the 250th anniversary of American independence, how do Europeans see Trump and the US?
entry.content: Views of the US are shifting across Europe, with confidence in Donald Trump and America's reliability eroding over time. How do individual European countries compare?

entry.title: Ancient Rome's version of Google Maps: how long to reach the beach
entry.content: A digital tool lets users explore the Roman Empire’s road network and, using historical data, estimate how long journeys between cities took 2,000 years ago.

entry.title: Ancient Rome's 'Google Maps' is here and working: Omnesviae
entry.content: A new digital tool lets users explore the Roman Empire’s road network and, using historical data, estimate travel times between cities 2,000 years ago.

entry.title: Don't block Ukraine's path to EU accession, Zelenskyy asks Hungary and Poland
entry.content: Facing resistance from Hungary and pushback from Poland, Volodymyr Zelenskyy said accession should be based on "rules" rather than "emotions".

entry.title: Newsletter: EU enlargement climbs the political agenda
entry.content: Good morning, I’m Mared Gwyn. Today: An exclusive on Brussels' planned redistribution of Western Balkans funding, why enlargement dominated the opening of Ireland's EU Council presidency, and a preview of the EU budget conference kicking off in Brussels.

entry.title: AirTags in luggage actually helps, with a 90% reduction of truly lost bags in a year
entry.content: Using a location tracking device has reduced recovery times by 26%, according to the SITA 2026 Baggage IT Insights.

entry.title: All members of Venezuelan nu-metal band Van Der Dijs confirmed dead in devastating earthquakes
entry.content: The band Van Der Dijs were in their rehearsal space at the time of the natural disaster.

entry.title: Europe Today: Clash over EU cash set to dominate today's Budget Conference
entry.content: The battle over the EU's next long-term budget will be on full display at today's Annual EU Budget Conference as member states clash over spending priorities, Ireland takes over the Presidency of the Council of the European Union, and we speak to the EU's head of aid operations in Venezuela.

entry.title: EXCLUSIVE: EU set to reallocate Western Balkans funding in favour of accession 'frontrunners'
entry.content: The EU will redirect Western Balkans reform funding towards frontrunners Montenegro, Albania and North Macedonia, as other countries are struggling to keep up with reform deadlines.

entry.title: Latest news bulletin | July 2nd, 2026 – Morning
entry.content: Catch up with the most important stories from around Europe and beyond this July 2nd, 2026 - latest news, breaking news, World, Business, Entertainment, Politics, Culture, Travel.

entry.title: Russia's petrol crisis worsens as citizens feel the bite of Moscow's war in Ukraine
entry.content: A third of Russia's refining capacity has been knocked out by Ukrainian drone attacks, causing petrol shortages across more than half of Russia's regions.

entry.title: Travelling to Croatia? Here are the best places to visit beyond the tourism hotspots
entry.content: Looking to discover Croatia beyond the headline hotspots? Euronews Travel asked the country’s tourist board which under-the-radar destinations travellers should visit and the events worth planning a trip around.

entry.title: US, Belgium and England all survive tough tests to reach World Cup Round of 16
entry.content: England and Belgium narrowly escaped shock Round of 32 eliminations on Wednesday. Harry Kane's late double rescued England in a 2–1 win over DR Congo, while Belgium overturned a 2–0 deficit to edge Senegal 3–2 in extra time.

entry.title: Is talking about 'slow tourism' elitist, or just the sector's latest trend?
entry.content: Travel platforms are rushing to praise slow travel as the must‑try trend for 2026, citing its potential environmental and psychological benefits. But can everyone afford it?

entry.title: Venezuela death toll rises to 2,295 as medical crisis widens
entry.content: Doctors fear a severe medical crisis in Venezuela following the 24 June earthquakes that killed 2,295 and injured more than 11,000. Thousands of displaced survivors are living in crowded shelters without clean water.

entry.title: US-Iran parallel talks in Qatar end with pledge to keep talking
entry.content: Holding separate meetings in Qatar, the US and Iran made progress and agreed that the next meeting would be after Khamenei's funeral, host Qatar confirmed.

entry.title: Russian missile and drone strikes kill 13 in Kyiv after Zelensky warns of massive attack
entry.content: "During the night, the enemy once again launched a massive attack on the Kyiv region using strike drones, ballistic missiles and cruise missiles," regional governor Mykola Kalashnyk said on Telegram.

entry.title: Committee tasked with post-war Gaza governance says Cyprus talks 'highly productive'
entry.content: Participants reviewed plans for reconstruction, security and governance, while developing a framework to ensure transparency and accountability for international donors.

entry.title: EU looks to South Caucasus for solutions as von der Leyen holds talks with President Aliyev
entry.content: Von der Leyen begins her South Caucasus tour in Baku as the EU steps up engagement on regional stability, energy and connectivity.

entry.title: Two arrested after unfurling 'power of love' banner on Empire State Building antenna
entry.content: Office workers wondered how the pair managed to get to the antenna of a high-profile building where visitors are screened and told not to bring large packages, sports equipment, costumes or masks, among other items.

entry.title: California to institute Bruce Lee Day, first for a Chinese-American in state history
entry.content: In the 1960s, Lee found work in Hollywood, notably as Kato in the TV series "The Green Hornet," but studios wanted him to play racist stereotypes and paid him less than his white counterparts.

entry.title: Nicolás Maduro sued in US court over alleged extrajudicial killings in Venezuela
entry.content: Maduro is currently in a New York jail awaiting trial on criminal charges of drug trafficking after the US military removed him from office in an extraordinary raid in Venezuela in January.

entry.title: 'England's superstar': Harry Kane the hero as Three Lions survive shock against DR Congo
entry.content: Thomas Tuchel’s England are through to the World Cup round of 16 after two late goals from captain Harry Kane saw the Three Lions come back to beat DR Congo 2-1.

entry.title: Ukraine launches 'transparent mechanism' allowing partner countries to buy weapons, Kyiv says
entry.content: According to the most recent reports, Ukraine is now producing over 4 million drones annually and could double that number with sufficient funding.

entry.title: MEPs clash in The Ring over the right answers to Europe's scorching heat dome
entry.content: A record-breaking heatwave is baking Europe - have politicians failed to protect citizens? Is air-conditioning the answer? In this edition, Benedetta Scuderi (Greens) and Andrea Wechsler (EPP) talk about the political consequences of a (failed?) climate policy.

entry.title: Man charged with sabotage of Nord Stream gas pipeline in German court
entry.content: Nord Stream is a network of pipelines running under the Baltic Sea from Russia to Germany to provide Western Europe with natural gas, meaning it provided billions to the Kremlin's coffers.

entry.title: Albania NATO summit hangs in balance as US slams security 'free riders'
entry.content: The proposed host country of next year's meeting hasn't shown credible progress on the alliance's new defence spending target of 5 percent of GDP.

entry.title: Planned 1.7 million satellites 'devastating' for astronomy by making night sky brighter
entry.content: There are also concerns that huge amounts of space debris from satellites could increasingly crash into each other in a dangerous chain reaction known as "Kessler syndrome."

entry.title: European Parliament to start procedure to ban Alternative for Germany’s EU party
entry.content: Most of the political groups are expected to approve a process that could lead to stripping the far-right Europe of Sovereign Nations of its status as a European political party and consequent funding.

entry.title: Trial begins for suspected mastermind behind killing of Maltese journalist Daphne Caruana Galizia
entry.content: Caruana Galizia had exposed corruption at the highest level in the country, shining a spotlight on murky links between Malta's business and political elites.

entry.title: Latest news bulletin | July 1st, 2026 – Evening
entry.content: Catch up with the most important stories from around Europe and beyond this July 1st, 2026 - latest news, breaking news, World, Business, Entertainment, Politics, Culture, Travel.

entry.title: Bayeux Tapestry in London: up to nine-hour wait to book tickets
entry.content: The British Museum website, where the tapestry will be on display from September 2026 to July 2027, has attracted tens of thousands of visitors keen to buy tickets.

entry.title: Bayeux Tapestry in London: queues of up to nine hours to book tickets
entry.content: The British Museum website, where the tapestry will be on display from September 2026 to July 2027, has attracted tens of thousands of visitors keen to buy tickets.

entry.title: 20 million children use AI and adopt it faster than adults, UNICEF says
entry.content: New UNICEF analysis finds children are adopting AI faster than adults, with millions turning to it for homework help and, in some cases, personal advice — as safeguards for young users lag badly behind.

entry.title: Russian gas imports rise despite EU phase-out
entry.content: Russian liquefied natural gas continues to arrive mainly through Spain, France, Belgium and the Netherlands, while pipeline deliveries remain concentrated in Hungary, Slovakia and Greece.

entry.title: England breaks record for warmest June since records began, Met Office says
entry.content: Scientists warn that extreme weather events such as heatwaves are becoming more frequent and intense as a result of human-induced climate change.

entry.title: Ukraine’s former commander-in-chief to run for president, reports say
entry.content: Valerii Zaluzhnyi, former commander-in-chief of Ukraine's Armed Forces and now Kyiv’s envoy to the UK, is reportedly willing to run in the next presidential election once the situation allows. Zaluzhnyi is seen as Zelenskyy’s main rival.

entry.title: 'Εβίβα!' It's official: Europe's Best Bar is in Greece
entry.content: The inaugural list of Europe’s 50 Best Bars has been revealed, and it was a great night for the Greek capital Athens...

entry.title: Firebomb attacks in northern Greece target residences of governing party members
entry.content: Prime Minister Kyriakos Mitsotakis expressed "outrage and anger" at what he called a "cowardly, terrorist and murderous attack."

entry.title: Finnish entrepreneur jailed for breach of EU sanctions after exporting trucks to Russia
entry.content: Finland's 1,340-kilometre border with Russia has been closed since December 2023 after the arrival of around 1,000 migrants without visas.

entry.title: Just a few social media posts from self-styled 'experts' can strongly influence opinion, study says
entry.content: Researchers warn social media users can form strong opinions after viewing only a few social media posts on a topic. Across social media, many self-proclaimed experts and influencers are plugging a gap amid declining public trust in traditional experts.

entry.title: O Canada! Eurovision Song Contest welcomes first new country since 2015
entry.content: Canada will sing at next Eurovision Song Contest after the country's public broadcaster confirmed full membership of the European Broadcasting Union - allowing them to compete in the world's biggest competition of its kind.

entry.title: Mattel releases new Barbie in partnership with Miley Cyrus
entry.content: Mattel releases yet another celebrity Barbie - this time with the American singer-songwriter and actress Miley Cyrus.
